definitions.technical: A protocol by which distributed nodes reach agreement on the current state of a shared ledger without relying on a central authority. Consensus mechanisms must balance safety (no conflicting states finalized), liveness (the system continues producing blocks), and fault tolerance (resilience to node failures or adversarial behavior).
definitions.lay: The method a blockchain uses to get all its computers to agree on which transactions are valid. Different blockchains use different methods, each with tradeoffs between speed, energy use, and security.
definitions.parenthetical: (the method by which distributed network participants agree on the valid state of the ledger)
definitions.legal_significance: The consensus mechanism determines who validates transactions and how disputes are resolved. RSA 301-B:5(VII) defines 'consensus mechanism' specifically. The choice of mechanism affects regulatory classification: proof-of-work networks consume energy (environmental regulation), while proof-of-stake networks involve staking (potential securities implications).
